Google announced on Thursday that it’s rolling out a new way to explore the web with autonomous tech Mode, its conversational search experience. Now, when you’re using autonomous tech Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with intelligent systems Mode.

The goal is to make it easier to explore relevant websites, compare details, and ask follow-up questions while preserving the context of your search, the tech giant reports.

For example, if you want to purchase a new a coffee maker, you can describe what you’re looking for in autonomous tech Mode and get a range of options. Once you click on one, you can open the retailer’s website alongside autonomous tech Mode and ask specific questions, like “how easy is this to clean?” autonomous tech Mode will then use context from the page and from across the web to answer your questions.

Furthermore, the industry expects that “Our early testers loved that they didn’t have to constantly switch tabs to get help with a comprehensive article or a long video,” Google explained in a blog post. “And they found that having both Search and the web side-by-side helped them stay focused on their tasks while exploring useful web pages.”

Google also announced a new way to search across the Chrome tabs you’re already looking at.

On Chrome desktop or mobile, you can tap the new “plus” menu in the search box on the “New Tab” page or in artificial intelligence Mode, then select recent tabs to include them in your search. This means you can mix and match multiple tabs, images, or files and bring that context into your autonomous tech Mode searches.
